page: Amazon Web Services m8in.8xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized virtual machine instance. It is built on the Nitro hypervisor and powered by an Intel x86_64 processor. The instance features 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of dedicated system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

This instance type does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached Elastic Block Store (EBS) volumes. To support this, the m8in.8xlarge is optimized for EBS and network performance, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. This high-throughput network interface facilitates rapid data transfer between the instance and external storage or other network resources.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8in.8xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise workloads that require high network throughput without the need for local NVMe drives. The combination of a 3.9 GHz Intel CPU and 128.0 GB of memory makes it appropriate for general-purpose applications, database hosting, application servers, and mid-tier enterprise workloads that rely heavily on network-attached storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m8in.8xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor, it features 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, which is supported by a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. This hardware profile makes the m8in.8xlarge highly suitable for workloads requiring a balance of compute and memory alongside high network throughput. It is well-suited for application servers, database hosting, containerized microservices, and enterprise workloads that utilize EBS volumes. The high-frequency processor and dedicated resource allocation ensure consistent performance for multi-threaded applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8in.8xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, a 3.9 GHz Intel processor, and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8in.8xlarge is an Intel-powered general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A network-optimized general-purpose instance featuring high-frequency Intel processors and robust baseline bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.

Equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps
Optimized for EBS and network-attached storage with no local storage
Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor for dedicated resource allocation
Suitable for application servers, database hosting, and network-heavy workloads
